perturbation.original_text: IX. The CONTRACTOR will return all documents, informat ion, knowledge, samples and data provided in the course of the cooperation including all copies thereof to the PURCHASER. In the case of retention of documents, information, knowledge, samples and data or copies by the CONTRACTOR or his assistants or agents, the PURCHASER may assert claim for damages.
perturbation.changed_text: IX. The CONTRACTOR will make reasonable efforts to return documents, information, knowledge, samples and data provided in the course of the cooperation including all copies thereof to the PURCHASER, unless retention is required by law or for internal record-keeping purposes. In the case of unreturned documents, information, knowledge, samples and data or copies by the CONTRACTOR or his assistants or agents, the PURCHASER may not assert claim for damages.
perturbation.explanation: The obligation to return all documents is weakened to 'make reasonable efforts', allowing exceptions for legal reasons and internal record-keeping without specifying limitations or approval. The phrase 'may assert claim for damages' is replaced with 'may not assert claim for damages'.This contradicts the strict return policy previously established, particularly regarding sensitive data, where any unauthorized retention could lead to legal issues.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IX. The CONTRACTOR will make reasonable efforts to return documents, information, knowledge, samples and data provided in the course of the cooperation including all copies thereof to the PURCHASER, unless retention is required by law or for internal record-keeping purposes. In the case of unreturned documents, information, knowledge, samples and data or copies by the CONTRACTOR or his assistants or agents, the PURCHASER may not assert claim for damages.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text from a strict requirement to return all documents and allows for exceptions (retention for legal reasons or internal record-keeping). Additionally, it changes the purchaser's ability to claim damages from 'may assert claim for damages' to 'may not assert claim for damages'. This directly contradicts the original intent of complete return and potential for damage claims, creating a clear conflict within the document according to the provided explanation and the Ambiguities definition, where obligations are defined inconsistently.

perturbation.original_text: X. The CONTRACTOR will observe the BDSG (Bundesdatens chutzgesetz = German Data Protection Act), regulations and other regulations for the data protection. In the case that the CONTRACTOR is granted access no matter of which kind by the PURCHASER to data for performance of the contractual works in te rms of the BDSG or made known such data by the PURCHASER, the CONTRACTOR will only con sign Employees, Assistants or Agents with the performance of the contractual work , who are obliged to data secrecy in terms of § 5 BDSG. On request of the PURCHASER, the CONTRACTOR will present the agreement made with the Employees, Assistants or Ag ents. The CONTRACTOR will not make available or known the data forwarded or made known by the PURCHASER in terms of the BDSG to third part ies, neither directly nor indirectly, and only use it to perform the work. After completion of the work, the CONTRACTOR will r eturn all data forwarded by the PURCHASER including the copies thereof to the PURCH ASER or completely delete the data including the copies thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: X. The CONTRACTOR will endeavor to observe the BDSG (Bundesdatens chutzgesetz = German Data Protection Act), regulations and other regulations for the data protection. In the case that the CONTRACTOR is granted access no matter of which kind by the PURCHASER to data for performance of the contractual works in te rms of the BDSG or made known such data by the PURCHASER, the CONTRACTOR will attempt to only con sign Employees, Assistants or Agents with the performance of the contractual work , who are generally made aware of data secrecy principles. On request of the PURCHASER, the CONTRACTOR will provide a summary of the awareness training provided to Employees, Assistants or Ag ents. The CONTRACTOR may make available or known the data forwarded or made known by the PURCHASER in terms of the BDSG to subcontractors, and only use it to perform the work. After completion of the work, the CONTRACTOR will endeavor to return all data forwarded by the PURCHASER including the copies thereof to the PURCH ASER or completely delete the data including the copies thereof, unless a longer retention period is required for regulatory compliance.
perturbation.explanation: This changes 'will observe' to 'will endeavor to observe' and 'will only consign Employees' to 'will attempt to only consign Employees', weakening data protection commitments. It also now permits data to be shared with subcontractors, a direct contradiction to the original restriction, and makes deletion of data conditional. These changes are conflicting given the previous strict regulations regarding data privacy as required from the German Data Protection Act.
perturbation.contradicted_text: X. The CONTRACTOR will endeavor to observe the BDSG (Bundesdatens chutzgesetz = German Data Protection Act), regulations and other regulations for the data protection. In the case that the CONTRACTOR is granted access no matter of which kind by the PURCHASER to data for performance of the contractual works in te rms of the BDSG or made known such data by the PURCHASER, the CONTRACTOR will attempt to only con sign Employees, Assistants or Agents with the performance of the contractual work , who are generally made aware of data secrecy principles. On request of the PURCHASER, the CONTRACTOR will provide a summary of the awareness training provided to Employees, Assistants or Ag ents. The CONTRACTOR may make available or known the data forwarded or made known by the PURCHASER in terms of the BDSG to subcontractors, and only use it to perform the work. After completion of the work, the CONTRACTOR will endeavor to return all data forwarded by the PURCHASER including the copies thereof to the PURCH ASER or completely delete the data including the copies thereof, unless a longer retention period is required for regulatory compliance.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces multiple contradictions. First, it weakens the commitment to data protection by changing 'will observe' to 'will endeavor to observe' and 'will only consign Employees' to 'will attempt to only consign Employees'. Second, it allows data to be shared with subcontractors, directly contradicting the original restriction. Finally, it makes the deletion of data conditional, which conflicts with the initial strict requirement to return or delete all data. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, showing the contradiction exists within that text itself by the inclusion of the above listed changes, which go against data privacy as required from the German Data Protection Act, as detailed in the perturbation explanation. This makes a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: XI. In the case of violating the obligations mentioned above, the CONTRACTOR will pay a contract penalty for every case of violation agains t one of the obligations taken over in the amount of Seite 3/3 Euro xxx.xxx (in words: EUR xxx thousand). The assertion of the actually occurred damage remai ns untouched, while the forfeited contract penalty is deducted.
perturbation.changed_text: XI. In the case of intentionally violating the core obligations mentioned above, the CONTRACTOR may be required to pay a contract penalty for significant violations in the amount of Seite 3/3 Euro xxx.xxx (in words: EUR xxx thousand) based on a court order. The assertion of the actually occurred damage remains the sole remedy, and the contract penalty will only be applied if the damage exceeds the penalty amount.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase, 'In the case of violating the obligations mentioned above' has now been changed to 'In the case of intentionally violating the core obligations mentioned above', which creates a higher standard of proof for violations of the core obligations. Also, by introducing conditions based on court order, contradicts the simple assessment of penalties. Now there is a higher threshhold and that creates incosistency with the nature and enforcement of penalties throughout the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XI. In the case of intentionally violating the core obligations mentioned above, the CONTRACTOR may be required to pay a contract penalty for significant violations in the amount of Seite 3/3 Euro xxx.xxx (in words: EUR xxx thousand) based on a court order. The assertion of the actually occurred damage remains the sole remedy, and the contract penalty will only be applied if the damage exceeds the penalty amount.
perturbation.justification: The original text implies a straightforward penalty for violating obligations. The changed text introduces a higher standard ('intentionally violating core obligations'), requires a court order, and makes the penalty contingent on the damage exceeding the penalty amount. This contradicts the original intent of easy penalty assessment. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text; however, the shift from the original text to the changed text introduces an in-text contradiction, as it now conflicts with a simpler understanding and enforcement of penalties throughout the rest of the document.
